title: Clarkia gracilis
taxonomy: Clarkia gracilis is a species of flowering plant in the evening primrose family (Onagraceae). It is a member of the genus Clarkia, which is a group of plants commonly known as clarkias or godetias. The genus Clarkia is further classified within the family Onagraceae, which also includes other plants such as evening primroses and fuchsia. The classification of Clarkia gracilis is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Myrtales, family Onagraceae, genus Clarkia, species C. gracilis. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics of Clarkia gracilis with other plants in its family and genus.
uses: Clarkia gracilis is not widely documented for its medicinal uses, but some species within the Clarkia genus are known for their potential health benefits. However, Clarkia gracilis, also known as the slender clarkia, may have some traditional uses in herbal medicine. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Clarkia gracilis for medicinal purposes.
